name: Prairie Fire
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a large pan.
2. Add the onions and sauté them until they are soft.
3. Stir in the bacon drippings.
4. Add the beans and mash them, incorporating bean juice if necessary.
5. Cook over low heat, continuing to mash and stir until the desired consistency is reached.
6. Incorporate the remaining ingredients.
7. Stir over low heat until the cheese has melted.
8. Taste for seasonings; add reserved jalapeno juice if needed.
9. Serve in a chafing dish with tostados or corn chips. This recipe can be frozen or stored in the refrigerator. It is best made ahead of time and reheated on low heat before serving.
